name: weekly-digests
tagline: Splits a claude-mem project timeline into per-ISO-week narrative chapters, written by sequential subagents that hand off a carry-forward blo
verdict: No security concerns: the only network call is a curl to the user's own local claude-mem worker, no secrets are read or requested, no piped-shell execution, no hidden or manipulative instructions. Quality and hygiene are otherwise solid (clear triggers, negative trigger, error handling, worked examples), but the Step 6 rename loop is not re-run safe -- runni

highlights.summary: Step 6's rename loop (line 200: `mv "$f" "${prefix}-$f"`) prefixes every *.md file in the digests directory with no check for an already-applied prefix; re-running the pipeline on the same project (e.

python3 (line 67), node (line 34), and curl (line 58) are used as hard dependencies, but Prerequisites (lines 26-29) lists only 'claude-mem worker running' and an output directory -- these tools are n
No version field in frontmatter and no changelog or dated revision notes anywhere in the body, so consumers can't tell when behavior last changed.
